---
title: "Variable Selection and Correlation"
author: "Carter Sevick"
date: "2026-04-26"
output: html_document
---
 
 
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(MASS)
library(ISLR)
library(tidyverse)

# Example data from the ISLR package
Hitters = na.omit(Hitters)

```

```{r}

# this is an explanatory function and is not guaranteed to work with all cases
# Example: only works with numeric variables

 

back_select <- function(data, vars, outcome, crit = 0.15) {
  
  tvars <- vars
  
  for(i in 1:length(tvars)){
    
    tfit <- lm(as.formula(paste(outcome, '~', paste(tvars, collapse='+'))), data = data)

    parms <- as.data.frame(summary(tfit)$coefficients) 
    parms <- within(parms, {
               parm <- row.names(parms)
             }) |> 
      base::subset(parm != '(Intercept)' )
    # get row with largest p-value
    parms <- parms[max(parms$`Pr(>|t|)`) == parms$`Pr(>|t|)`, ]
    
    # test against critical value
    parms <- parms[parms$`Pr(>|t|)` > crit, ]
    
    # what to do?
    if(nrow(parms) > 1) {
      message('Error: more than one variable selected for exclusion')
      message(parms)
    } else if(nrow(parms) == 1) {
        message(paste('Step'
                  , as.character(i)
                  ,': ',parms$parm
                  , 'has been excluded from the model: p-value = '
                  , as.character(round(parms$`Pr(>|t|)`, 4))))
      
      # remove variable with highest p-value, if above the crit threshold
      tvars <- setdiff(tvars, parms$parm)
      
      # check if all variables have been excluded
      if(length(tvars) == 0 ) {
        message(paste('Step'
                    , as.character(i)
                    ,': No variables selected into the model'))
        break
      }
    } else {
      message(paste('Step'
                  , as.character(i)
                  ,': Selection complete'
                  ,'final model:'
                  ,paste(tvars, collapse = ', ')
      ))
      break
    } 
  }
  
  if(length(tvars > 0)) {return(tvars)} else {return(character(0))}
}

```

Backward selection example
```{r}
back_select(Hitters,
c('AtBat','Hits','HmRun','Runs','RBI','Walks','Years','CAtBat','CHits','CHmRun','CRuns','CRBI','CWalks',
'PutOuts','Assists','Errors')
,'Salary')
```


Simulation data of a normal outcome allowing multiple predictors, possibly correlated
```{r}
sim_dat <- function( n = 189 # size of each simulation dataset
                     , B = c(-1.0871, 0.7041, 0)# true model parameters
                     , cov_mu = c(0, 0)  # means of x variables
                     , cov_var = diag(2) # covariance matrix of X variables
                     , e_var = 1
                     ) {
  
  # add generation of multiple, possibly correlated variables using the multivariate normal distribution 
  x <- MASS::mvrnorm(n=n, mu = cov_mu, Sigma = cov_var)

  x_matrix <- cbind(intercept = rep(1, n), x)
  
  v = as.vector(x_matrix %*% B)
  y = sapply(v, function(x){rnorm(1, mean = x, sd = sqrt(e_var))})
  
  simdat <- as.data.frame(cbind(y, x))
  vnames <- c('y',paste0('x',1:length(cov_mu)))
  names(simdat) <- vnames
  
  return(simdat)
}
dat = sim_dat()

summary(lm(y~x1+x2, data = dat))
```

```{r}

back_select(sim_dat(), c('x1','x2'), 'y')

```
 
 
two equally strong predictors, uncorrelated
```{r}
nsim <- 10000
res <- rep(NA, nsim)
res2 <- list()
for(i in 1:nsim) {
dat <- sim_dat(B = c(-1, 0.5, 0.5), cov_mu=c(0,0), cov_var = matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), 2, 2), e_var = 4)
tmp <-
  suppressMessages(
    back_select(dat
            , c('x1','x2')
            , 'y'
            )
    )
  res[i] <- length(tmp)
  res2[[i]] <- tmp
}
table(res)/length(res)
```

two equally strong predictors, CORRELATED!!!

```{r}
nsim <- 10000
res <- rep(NA, nsim)
res2 <- list()
for(i in 1:nsim) {
dat <- sim_dat(B = c(-1, 0.5, 0.5), cov_mu=c(0,0), cov_var = matrix(c(1, 0.75, 0.75, 1), 2, 2), e_var = 4)
tmp <-
  suppressMessages(
    back_select(dat
            , c('x1','x2')
            , 'y'
            )
    )
  res[i] <- length(tmp)
  res2[[i]] <- tmp
}
table(res)/length(res)
```


```{r}
nsim <- 10000
res <- rep(NA, nsim)
res2 <- list()
coef_x1 <- rep(NA, nsim)  # ADD THIS

for(i in 1:nsim) {
  dat <- sim_dat(B = c(-1, 0.5, 0.5), cov_mu=c(0,0), 
                 cov_var = matrix(c(1, 0.75, 0.75, 1), 2, 2), e_var = 4)
  
  tmp <- suppressMessages(back_select(dat, c('x1','x2'), 'y'))
  res[i] <- length(tmp)
  res2[[i]] <- tmp
  
  # ADD THIS: fit model with selected variables and grab x1 coefficient
  if("x1" %in% tmp) {
    fit <- lm(as.formula(paste("y ~", paste(tmp, collapse = "+"))), data = dat)
    coef_x1[i] <- coef(fit)["x1"]
  }
}

table(res)/length(res)

# Bias: split x1 estimates by whether x2 was also selected or not
mean(coef_x1[res == 2], na.rm = TRUE)  # x1 estimate when BOTH variables kept
mean(coef_x1[res == 1], na.rm = TRUE)  # x1 estimate when x2 was DROPPED


```
